channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Brief Review: Rethinking Colonic Redundancy in Gastroenterology.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Review - Dolichocolon (DC), or colonic redundancy, is an elongated and tortuous colon described as early as 1820, yet it remains underrecognized in clinical gastroenterology. Advances in imaging and motilit...

item.title: Atopic dermatitis.
item.description: Nature reviews. Disease primers - Review - Atopic dermatitis (AD) is the most common inflammatory skin disease and carries the highest disability-adjusted life-years burden, ranking 15th among all non-fatal diseases globally. It is characte...

item.title: A novel deep learning network for small bowel ulcerative lesion detection and differential diagnosis on double-balloon endoscopy images.
item.description: Biomedical physics & engineering express - Differentiating small bowel ulcerative diseases (SBUDs) on double-balloon endoscopy (DBE) is challenging. We aimed to develop an artificial intelligence (AI) model using DBE images for accurate SBU...

item.title: Early-life gastrointestinal inflammation and the developing brain: Unravelling the pathways to long-term cognitive dysfunction.
item.description: World journal of clinical pediatrics - Review - The gut-brain axis (GBA) is a complex, bidirectional communication network critical to integrating central nervous system functions with gastrointestinal (GI) health. This review examines how disru...

item.title: Epigenetic reprogramming of tissue-resident memory T cells in chronic inflammatory disorders and implications for targeted therapies.
item.description: Epigenomics - Tissue-resident memory T (TRM) cells play a role in causing long-term tissue injury in chronic inflammatory diseases via pathological epigenetic reprogramming. Nevertheless, the epigenetic processe...

item.title: The role and targeting potential analysis of angiogenesis-related target THY1 in DSS-induced acute colitis in mice.
item.description: PloS one - Immune-driven inflammatory angiogenesis plays a crucial role in the pathogenesis of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). However, the mechanism of chronic inflammation mediated by angiogenesis still r...

item.title: Liver Injury From Medications Used for Treating Inflammatory Bowel Disease: The Drug-Induced Liver Injury Network Experience.
item.description: Liver international : official journal of the International Association for the Study of the Liver - We aimed to investigate and characterise DILI due to approved therapies for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Using the Drug-Induced Liver Injury Network (DILIN) prospective study, we evaluated def...

item.title: A phase 1 study to evaluate the safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics of PF-07261271 in healthy participants.
item.description: British journal of clinical pharmacology - There is an unmet need for more effective therapies in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). A single drug that blocks multiple distinct pathogenic pathways may offer therapeutic benefit superior to cu...

item.title: Beta-Glucan modulates monocyte plasticity and differentiation capacity to mitigate DSS-induced colitis.
item.description: eLife - Trained immunity involves the reprogramming of innate immune cells after an initial exposure, resulting in heightened inflammatory responses to subsequent stimuli and enhanced bactericidal capacity...

item.title: Abbreviated non-contrast magnetic resonance enterography for Crohn's disease: a reliability study.
item.description: La Radiologia medica - To evaluate the diagnostic reliability and time efficiency of an abbreviated non-contrast magnetic resonance enterography (MRE) protocol for assessing Crohn's disease activity. This retrospective s...

item.title: The Association of Faecal Calprotectin Measurements With Endo-Histological Remission in Ulcerative Colitis: A Pooled Analysis.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - Endoscopic and histologic evaluation is part of routine clinical practice to confirm the presence of active disease in ulcerative colitis. Faecal calprotectin (FCAL) levels show a positive correlat...

item.title: Impact of Clostridioides difficile Infection on Clinical Outcomes in Hospitalized Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Journal of clinical gastroenterology -

item.title: Corrigendum to 'Dynamic profiling in inflammatory Bowel disease: A manifesto for personalized care' [Autoimmunity Reviews 25 (2026) 104068].
item.description: Autoimmunity reviews -

item.title: Prevalence of multiple morbidities and cancers in individuals with Down syndrome: A matched descriptive study using linked electronic health record data.
item.description: PloS one - Down syndrome is associated with the development of multiple morbidities throughout the life course, yet comprehensive data on its relative burden remains limited. This descriptive, matched, retros...

item.title: Small but mighty: the human mycobiome as an immunopharmacological regulator of inflammation and autoimmunity.
item.description: International immunopharmacology - Review - The human mycobiome comprising commensal fungi such as Saccharomyces, Candida, Malassezia and others constitutes less than 0.1 percent of total gut microbes yet exerts outsized influence on host im...

item.title: The complement system in inflammatory bowel disease: from early observations to emerging frontiers.
item.description: Current opinion in gastroenterology - The complement system is one of the most evolutionarily conserved arms of innate immunity and has re-emerged as an important regulator of intestinal inflammation in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD)...

item.title: Cell-type-resolved genetic variation shapes inflammatory bowel disease risk.
item.description: Nature - Most genetic variants associated with complex diseases lie in non-coding regions1, complicating efforts to identify effector genes and relevant cell types. Here we map cis-expression quantitative t...

item.title: Position statement of the spanish working group on crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis (geteccu) on the diagnosis and management of acute severe ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Gastroenterologia y hepatologia - Acute severe ulcerative colitis (ASUC) is a potentially life-threatening medical emergency that requires hospitalization, close multidisciplinary monitoring, and prompt, protocolized decision-makin...

item.title: A Case of Epstein-Barr Virus Associated Smooth Muscle Tumor in the Bronchus of a Child with CARMIL2 Deficiency and a Literature Review.
item.description: Fetal and pediatric pathology - Background: Epstein-Barr virus associated smooth muscle tumors (EBV-SMTs) have been established as a rare neoplasm closely associated with severe immunodeficiency. CARMIL2 deficiency, described in ...

item.title: DOAC Pharmacokinetics in Malabsorptive States.
item.description: Cardiology in review - Direct oral anticoagulants, such as dabigatran, rivaroxaban, apixaban, and edoxaban, are becoming more popular for the prevention of stroke and treatment of venous thromboembolism, although their e...
